In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|qq=}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

{{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.

It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)

== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

revision.text:
{{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|qid=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|qid=Q22120745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]

== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]

According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.

[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.

=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)

According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11611064]] Wikidata name: Shōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]] (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Sect]] Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] standing statue, designated as a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]].
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]], as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day [[:en:Tanzan Shrine|Tanzan Shrine]])<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]]. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Heijō Shrine]]'s [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the [[:en:Sakurai Line|JR Sakurai Line]] or [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] at [[:en:Sakurai Station|Sakurai Station (Nara)]], followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]

[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} standing statue, designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}}.
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day {{ill|Tanzan Shrine|en|Tanzan Shrine|WD=Q488618|lt=Tanzan Shrine}})<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji}}. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Heijō Shrine}}'s {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}} and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=JR Sakurai Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}} at {{ill|Sakurai Station|en|Sakurai Station|WD=Q1205698|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)}}, followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745}}}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}} (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect}} Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} standing statue, designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan}}.
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day {{ill|Tanzan Shrine|en|Tanzan Shrine|WD=Q488618|lt=Tanzan Shrine}})<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji}}. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Heijō Shrine}}'s {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji}} and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=JR Sakurai Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}} at {{ill|Sakurai Station|en|Sakurai Station|WD=Q1205698|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)}}, followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212|1=Jōe}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|1=Kṣitigarbha}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745|1=Yamato Northern 88 Sacred Sites}}}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} standing statue, designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}.
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day {{ill|Tanzan Shrine|en|Tanzan Shrine|WD=Q488618|lt=Tanzan Shrine|1=Tanzan Shrine}})<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}}. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Heijō Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}'s {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=JR Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} at {{ill|Sakurai Station|en|Sakurai Station|WD=Q1205698|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|1=Sakurai Station}}, followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shōrin-ji (Sakurai)]][[ja:聖林寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.491694|N|135.851833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212|1=Jōe}}|deity={{ill|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|1=Kṣitigarbha}}|part_of={{ill|Q22120745|WD=Q22120745|1=Yamato Northern 88 Sacred Sites}}}} {{Temple infobox Japan | name = Shōrin-ji | image = [[File:Shorinji03s3200.jpg|260px]] | caption = Shōrin-ji Temple | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|29|30.1|N|135|51|6.6|E|scale:10000_region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | mountain_name = Ryōonzan (霊園山) | sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Murō-ji Branch | principal = Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu | established = Traditional date: Wadō 5 (712) | founder = Trad. {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}} | designation = {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} (Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon statue) | website = [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website] }}
[[File:Shorinji06s3200.jpg|thumb|198px|Temple Courtyard]] '''Shōrin-ji''' (聖林寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Murō-ji branch Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Ryōonzan (霊園山), and its principal image is the Koyasu Enmei Jizō Bosatsu. Shōrin-ji is renowned for housing the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} standing statue, designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}.
== History == Shōrin-ji is situated on a small elevated area south of central Sakurai, overlooking the northern part of the Nara Basin. According to tradition, it was founded in Wadō 5 (712) by {{ill|Jōe|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōe|qq=}}, the eldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, as a branch temple of Myōraku-ji (present-day {{ill|Tanzan Shrine|en|Tanzan Shrine|WD=Q488618|lt=Tanzan Shrine|1=Tanzan Shrine}})<ref name="#1">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, p.388</ref><ref name="#2">『国史大辞典』（吉川弘文館）「聖林寺」の項（執筆者: 堀池春峰, 水野敬三郎）</ref>.
The early history of Shōrin-ji is unclear, but records indicate that it was destroyed during the late Heian period when Myōraku-ji clashed with {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}}. The temple was subsequently restored in the Kamakura period.
During the Edo period, the temple was reestablished by Shōryō Genshin, who relocated structures from {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Heijō Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}'s {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} and maintained deep ties with {{ill|Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|ja|平等寺 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-ji (Sakurai)|qq=}}. In the mid-Edo period (Kyōhō era, 1716–1736), Shōrin-ji adopted its current name<ref name="#2"/>.
In the Meiji era, following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} policy, the Eleven-faced Kannon statue, previously housed at the {{ill|Daigorinji|ja|大御輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigorinji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was transferred to Shōrin-ji<ref name="#3">『日本歴史地名大系 奈良県の地名』, 平凡社, 1981, 11.388, 433, 437</ref><ref name="#4">『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝』9号, 朝日新聞社, 1997, 11.'''4'''-280 * '''4'''-281</ref><ref name="#5">『原寸大日本の仏像 奈良編』（講談社MOOK）, 講談社, 2008, 11.184, 192, 193</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Eleven-faced Kannon Shorinji.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Eleven-faced Kannon statue]]
=== National Treasures === * '''Wood-core dry lacquer Eleven-faced Kannon Standing Statue''' This statue, measuring 209.1 cm in height, is a prime example of Nara-period craftsmanship. It features elaborate dry lacquerwork over a wooden core and was transferred to Shōrin-ji during the Meiji period<ref name="#2"/><ref name="#5"/>.
=== Municipal Cultural Properties === * ''Fudaraku Mountain Pure Land Mandala'' * Simple-patterned Qing (bronze bell)
== Access == * Accessible via the {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=JR Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} or {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} at {{ill|Sakurai Station|en|Sakurai Station|WD=Q1205698|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|1=Sakurai Station}}, followed by a 5-minute walk from the "Shōrin-ji Mae" bus stop on the Sakurai City Community Bus (Tōnomine Line).
== External Links == * [http://www.shorinji-temple.jp/ Shōrin-ji Official Website]
== References ==
{{translated page|ja|聖林寺|version=104664288|comment=Imported full ja history}}
